# Wenlan

> Wenlan is a living personal knowledge library for AI work, built by agents and grounded in its sources. Hybrid retrieval publishes CE-reranker snapshots for LME_Oracle at 93.6% Recall@5 / 0.857 MRR / 0.883 NDCG@10 (500 Q) and LME_S at 87.7% Recall@5 / 0.815 MRR / 0.822 NDCG@10 (deep, N=90). Retrieval uses ~168 tokens per recall query versus full replay at 4,505 tokens per query. Real git versioning for readable pages, sessions, handoffs, and status artifacts under ~/.wenlan/.git/, mandatory provenance on source-cited wiki pages, and one daemon serving Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Claude Desktop, Gemini CLI, and other MCP-compatible AI tools. Cross-platform on macOS (arm64, x64), Linux (x86_64, aarch64; glibc), and Windows (x86_64). Apache-2.0. Source at github.com/7xuanlu/wenlan.

## Core

- [Wenlan homepage](https://useorigin.app/): What Wenlan is, who it is for, how it differs from cloud memory layers.
- [About](https://useorigin.app/about): Project background and the person behind Wenlan.
- [Get started](https://useorigin.app/docs/get-started): Install the Claude Code plugin or run Wenlan setup for another MCP client, then verify the first memory round trip locally.
- [Daily workflow](https://useorigin.app/docs/daily-workflow): Capture, handoff, distill. The session loop that makes AI work compound across days.
- [Capture quality](https://useorigin.app/docs/capture-quality): What to capture, what to skip, and how corrections, review, distill, and forget fit the memory loop.
- [Review and trust](https://useorigin.app/docs/review-and-trust): How pending captures, revisions, contradictions, quality-gate rejections, confirm, and forget fit before memory becomes trusted context.
- [Core concepts](https://useorigin.app/docs/core-concepts): Memories, pages, entities, sessions, sources. The vocabulary the daemon enforces.
- [Memory types](https://useorigin.app/docs/memory-types): Wenlan's six canonical memory_type values, stability tiers, aliases, and how agents should choose between identity, preference, decision, lesson, gotcha, and fact.
- [Glossary](https://useorigin.app/docs/glossary): Short definitions for Wenlan terms such as memory, handoff, page, space, daemon, MCP, local index, provenance, supersession, and retrieval metrics.
- [Architecture](https://useorigin.app/docs/architecture): Daemon-first system map: wenlan-server, wenlan-core, wenlan CLI, wenlan-mcp, wenlan-types, and the Claude Code plugin.

## Concepts

- [AI work memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-work-memory): What changes when AI sessions can carry context across tools and weeks.
- [MCP memory server](https://useorigin.app/learn/mcp-memory-server): How Wenlan exposes memory through the Model Context Protocol so any compatible AI client can read and write the same store.
- [Local-first AI work memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/local-first-ai-memory): Data ownership, privacy, control. Why the daemon writes to ~/.wenlan/ instead of the cloud.
- [Markdown plus local index](https://useorigin.app/learn/markdown-local-index-ai-memory): The storage model. Markdown projection for humans, libSQL index for retrieval, both kept in sync.
- [Distilled wiki pages](https://useorigin.app/learn/distilled-wiki-pages-ai-memory): Why Wenlan composes atomic captures into source-backed pages with provenance and refreshable revision state.
- [Claude Code memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-code-memory): How Wenlan works inside Claude Code sessions and how /capture and /handoff fit the work loop.
- [Claude Code /memory vs Wenlan](https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-code-memory-command-vs-wenlan): When native Claude Code memory is enough and when Wenlan adds source-backed local MCP memory.
- [AI agent handoff loop](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-agent-handoff-loop): Session-end discipline that prevents context loss between AI sessions.
- [Wenlan for Claude Code](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-for-claude-code): The practical /init, /brief, /capture, /handoff, and /distill loop for Claude Code users.
- [Where Wenlan stores Claude Code memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/where-wenlan-stores-claude-code-memory): Local ~/.wenlan artifacts, pages, sessions, and git history.
- [How to add memory to Claude Code](https://useorigin.app/learn/how-to-add-memory-to-claude-code): Plugin install, /init, and first capture/recall verification.
- [How to give Codex persistent memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/how-to-give-codex-persistent-memory): MCP-only setup for Codex with Wenlan.
- [How to add MCP memory to Cursor](https://useorigin.app/learn/how-to-add-mcp-memory-to-cursor): Cursor MCP setup and verification loop.
- [Claude Desktop MCP memory setup](https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-desktop-mcp-memory-setup): Connect Claude Desktop to Wenlan through MCP.
- [AI agent memory: local vs cloud](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-agent-memory-local-vs-cloud): Choose the right trust boundary for AI memory.
- [Why AI coding agents lose context](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-coding-agent-loses-context): The session-boundary problem Wenlan solves.
- [Persistent project context for AI agents](https://useorigin.app/learn/persistent-project-context-for-ai-agents): Decisions, lessons, constraints, and handoffs across tools.
- [MCP memory server on localhost 7878](https://useorigin.app/learn/mcp-memory-server-localhost-7878): Wenlan daemon boundary and setup debugging.
- [What to capture in AI work memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/what-to-capture-in-ai-work-memory): Capture quality rules for durable AI work context.

## Comparisons

- [AI work memory vs knowledge base](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-work-memory-vs-knowledge-base): Documents and notes vs session memory, handoffs, retrieval, and agent reuse.
- [Wenlan vs Basic Memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-basic-memory): Markdown knowledge base vs AI work-session memory layer. Side-by-side dimensions plus a migration note.
- [Wenlan vs claude-mem](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-claude-mem): Observer-style Claude Code memory vs MCP-first cross-tool memory.
- [Wenlan vs Superlocal Memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-superlocal-memory): Honest benchmark framing, a 10-second inspectability test, and a two-week parallel-trial protocol for evaluating both.
- [Wenlan vs mcp-memory-service](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-mcp-memory-service): Local AI work memory vs self-hosted agent-pipeline memory backend.
- [Wenlan vs ChatGPT memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-chatgpt-memory): Built-in assistant personalization vs local cross-tool work memory.
- [Wenlan vs Obsidian for AI memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-obsidian-ai-memory): Obsidian vault workflows, obsidian-mind, claude-obsidian, Obsidian MCP bridges, and Wenlan's agent-facing work-memory loop.
- [Wenlan vs Notion AI](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-notion-ai): Team workspace AI vs local AI work memory.
- [Wenlan vs Mem0](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vs-mem0): App memory infrastructure vs personal local AI work memory.

## Workflows and Trust

- [Wenlan workflow for Codex](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-codex-workflow): Context, capture, recall, and handoff from Codex.
- [Wenlan workflow for Cursor](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-cursor-workflow): Cursor MCP memory workflow for repeated coding sessions.
- [Wenlan workflow for Claude Desktop](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-claude-desktop-workflow): Planning and research memories that later coding agents can reuse.
- [Wenlan workflow for Gemini CLI](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-gemini-cli-workflow): CLI memory workflow through MCP.
- [Wenlan workflow for VS Code MCP clients](https://useorigin.app/learn/wenlan-vscode-mcp-workflow): VS Code surfaces using Wenlan's local daemon.
- [Claude Code session handoff](https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-code-session-handoff): Use /handoff to preserve state for future sessions.
- [Shared memory between Cursor and Claude Code](https://useorigin.app/learn/cursor-claude-code-shared-memory): One local daemon across two coding tools.
- [Shared memory between Codex and Claude Code](https://useorigin.app/learn/codex-claude-code-shared-memory): Carry implementation context across Codex and Claude Code.
- [Multi-agent memory workflow](https://useorigin.app/learn/multi-agent-memory-workflow): Shared local memory for multiple AI clients.
- [AI agent project status handoff](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-agent-project-status-handoff): Durable status without bloating memory.
- [Source-backed wiki pages for AI work](https://useorigin.app/learn/source-backed-wiki-pages-ai-work): Readable pages with source memory IDs.
- [AI memory provenance](https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-memory-provenance): Why source IDs, revisions, and review matter.
- [Local git history for AI memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/local-git-history-ai-memory): Inspectable memory and page write history.
- [Review before trust in AI memory](https://useorigin.app/learn/review-before-trust-ai-memory): Keep uncertain, stale, or contradictory context visible.
- [What Wenlan is not](https://useorigin.app/learn/project-scope-ai-memory): Product boundaries for local AI work memory.

## Reference

- [Commands](https://useorigin.app/docs/commands): Wenlan CLI subcommands and the MCP tools the daemon exposes.
- [Claude Code plugin](https://useorigin.app/docs/claude-code-plugin): The richest Wenlan workflow inside Claude Code: /init, /brief, /capture, /recall, /handoff, /distill, /review, the SessionStart hook, and local memory mode.
- [CLI and service management](https://useorigin.app/docs/cli-and-service): Setup, daemon status, doctor, service install/uninstall, terminal recall/search/store, and MCP client wiring.
- [Updates and uninstall](https://useorigin.app/docs/updates-and-uninstall): Refresh the local runtime, verify with doctor, restart MCP clients, and remove the per-user service safely.
- [Upgrade notes](https://useorigin.app/docs/upgrade-notes): What to rerun, verify, and restart when Wenlan releases change the plugin, npm setup, MCP connector, daemon service, spaces, or platform support.
- [Packages and registries](https://useorigin.app/docs/packages-and-registries): Which package names mean what across the Claude Code plugin, npm setup, wenlan-mcp, wenlan-types, crates.io, and GitHub Releases.
- [Platform support](https://useorigin.app/docs/platforms): macOS, Linux, and Windows support, service managers, data paths, model backend notes, and Docker/VM caveats.
- [HTTP API](https://useorigin.app/docs/http-api): Local daemon surfaces that the plugin, CLI, MCP connector, and local tools call under the hood.
- [API examples](https://useorigin.app/docs/api-examples): Local curl examples for daemon health, setup status, memory store/search, chat context, review, confirm, and page search.
- [Typed clients](https://useorigin.app/docs/typed-clients): When Rust local tools should use wenlan-types for shared daemon request and response shapes instead of untyped JSON.
- [Spaces](https://useorigin.app/docs/spaces): Separate work, personal, client, and project memory buckets, including WENLAN_SPACE, spaces.toml mappings, resolver priority, and doctor output.
- [Knowledge graph](https://useorigin.app/docs/knowledge-graph): How people, projects, tools, observations, and relations become graph context for retrieval without replacing source-backed memory.
- [Source-backed pages](https://useorigin.app/docs/source-backed-pages): How atomic captures become readable Markdown pages with source memory IDs, revision state, and refresh paths.
- [Import and portability](https://useorigin.app/docs/import-and-portability): Keep Wenlan's projected Markdown artifacts readable outside the daemon and migrate selected context deliberately.
- [Local git history](https://useorigin.app/docs/local-git-history): Inspect the real git history Wenlan keeps under ~/.wenlan/.git for pages, sessions, handoffs, and status artifacts.
- [Models and keys](https://useorigin.app/docs/models-and-keys): No-model local memory mode, agent-side fallback phases, optional on-device models, optional Anthropic keys, and the privacy/capability tradeoffs.
- [Advanced retrieval status](https://useorigin.app/docs/advanced-retrieval): Stable hybrid retrieval plus opt-in main-branch retrieval experiments and their release-status caveats.
- [Experimental flags](https://useorigin.app/docs/experimental-flags): How to read opt-in main-branch flags for retrieval, context compression, memory maintenance, entity resolution, page guards, and reflection without treating them as released defaults.
- [Data and privacy](https://useorigin.app/docs/data-and-privacy): What lives where on disk, what leaves the machine, what does not.
- [Backup and migration](https://useorigin.app/docs/backup-and-migration): Back up readable ~/.wenlan artifacts and daemon data together, then verify restored installs with doctor and recall.
- [Configuration](https://useorigin.app/docs/configuration): Spaces, MCP client wiring, daemon bind address, local paths, models, and keys without hand-editing the database.
- [Environment variables](https://useorigin.app/docs/environment-variables): Runtime, development, Windows repair, and eval environment variables such as WENLAN_SPACE, WENLAN_HOST, WENLAN_BIND_ADDR, WENLAN_DATA_DIR, ORT_DYLIB_PATH, and EVAL_BASELINES_DIR.
- [MCP clients](https://useorigin.app/docs/mcp-clients): Setup notes for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Claude Desktop, Gemini CLI, and VS Code.
- [Agent profiles](https://useorigin.app/docs/agent-profiles): How Wenlan attributes captures to Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local scripts, and other clients through source_agent, trust, and enabled state.
- [Troubleshooting](https://useorigin.app/docs/troubleshooting): Common failure modes and the daemon repair paths.
- [Diagnostics and issue reports](https://useorigin.app/docs/diagnostics-and-issue-reports): Commands to run before asking for help, how to separate daemon and MCP failures, and what to redact.
- [FAQ](https://useorigin.app/docs/faq): Short answers about what Wenlan is, which install path to use, desktop app status, local data, Markdown artifacts, uninstall behavior, and verification.
- [Security and reporting](https://useorigin.app/docs/security): Private vulnerability reporting, redacted bug reports, local security boundaries, and security.txt.
- [Evaluation](https://useorigin.app/docs/evaluation): Retrieval benchmark scope, current LME_Oracle and LME_S snapshot, and rerun methodology.
- [Desktop app status](https://useorigin.app/docs/desktop-app): Whether the optional GUI is required, how the separate desktop repo fits, and what the daemon still owns.
- [Changelog](https://useorigin.app/docs/changelog): Stable release notes and how to read unreleased main-branch work.
- [Releases and versioning](https://useorigin.app/docs/releases-and-versioning): How release-please, tags, package publishing, npm packages, crates, binaries, and pre-1.0 version bumps work.
- [Roadmap and status](https://useorigin.app/docs/roadmap): Released, on-main, and planned work without confusing roadmap themes for shipped guarantees.
- [Project scope](https://useorigin.app/docs/project-scope): What Wenlan is for and what it deliberately avoids.
- [Build from source](https://useorigin.app/docs/build-from-source): Source build, workspace crates, platform notes, and contributor checks.
- [Testing and CI](https://useorigin.app/docs/testing-and-ci): Local checks, git hooks, PR CI, coverage policy, and manual eval boundaries for contributors.
- [Development conventions](https://useorigin.app/docs/development-conventions): Crate boundaries, SQL safety, async state snapshots, typed MCP wrappers, dev/prod isolation, and worktree gotchas for contributors.
- [Contributing](https://useorigin.app/docs/contributing): Development setup, useful issues, PR process, architecture rules, and license notes.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://useorigin.app/llms-full.txt): Long-form companion to this file. Contains the full body of every Learn article and Docs page so an LLM can ingest the full corpus in one fetch.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/7xuanlu/wenlan): Source, releases, issues. Apache-2.0.
- [Wenlan Claude Code plugin (npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/wenlan): The Claude Code plugin published from this repo.
- [wenlan-mcp server (npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/wenlan-mcp): The MCP server binary as a standalone npm package.
- [wenlan-mcp on crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/wenlan-mcp): The Rust crate behind wenlan-mcp.
- [wenlan-types on crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/wenlan-types): Shared wire types for downstream tools.
- [License](https://github.com/7xuanlu/wenlan/blob/main/LICENSE): Apache-2.0.
